images.caption: Figure 1:Geometric stability predicts neural-behavioral coupling; centroid drift does not.

Figure 2:Recurrent circuitry predicts geometric stability in
the olfactory hierarchy.Geometric stability (Shesha) in three groups from the
Bolding and Franks (2018) PCX-1 dataset.
OB: olfactory bulb recordings (n=11n=11);
TeLC PCx: piriform cortex with recurrent connections silenced
by tetanus toxin light chain (n=7n=7);
Control PCx: contralateral intact piriform cortex (n=5n=5).
Bars show mean with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals;
open circles show individual recordings.
The predicted ordering OB<<TeLC PCx<<Control PCx
is observed.
Bracket: Wilcoxon signed-rank test, Control vs. TeLC,p=0.16p=0.16(n.s.).

Figure 3:Recurrent coupling increases geometric stability via
pattern completion in a rate network model.
